slatePreview.slateOverview: Today's slate features strong HR environments at Yankee Stadium (1.18) and Great American Ball Park (1.25), where elevated park factors pair with favorable right-handed pitching matchups. Ben Rice and Paul Goldschmidt draw into Nick Martinez in a hitter-friendly park, while Sal Stewart and JJ Bleday benefit from Kyle Leahy's presence at GABP. Wrigley Field and Oracle Park suppress power, but the model still identifies Yordan Alvarez and Casey Schmitt as viable despite the neutral-to-pitcher venues.
slatePreview.modelNote: The model places heavy weight on barrel rate, hard-hit metrics, and recent HR trends, prioritizing players like Juan Soto and Michael Harris II who combine elite exit velocities with strong last-7 and last-30 HR counts.
